text: Conceptual approaches to illiberalism and the way they have come to be used in the scholarly literature are presented. An approach to the concept of illiberalism as the negation of the three fundamental principles of liberal democracy, namely the principle of the restriction of state power, the principle of the neutrality of the state and the principle of an open society is applied in the article. The methodology applied in the article and the consequent research possibilities are detailed in its second part. The interdependencies between populism, authoritarianism and illiberalism are analyzed. Furthermore, the possible ways to illiberalism are identified (1) authoritarian; (2) traditionalist; (3) religious; (4) libertarian; (5) nativist-nationalist; (6) populist; (7) paternalist; (8) materialist-technocratic; and (9) left-wing.
headline: Fully Human Written
